name: chatgpt-app-builder
description:
Build ChatGPT Apps using the Apps SDK and MCP. Use when users want to:
(1) Evaluate if their product should become a ChatGPT App
(2) Design and implement MCP servers with widgets
(3) Test apps locally and in ChatGPT
(4) Prepare for App Store submission
Triggers: "ChatGPT app", "Apps SDK", "build for ChatGPT",
"ChatGPT integration", "MCP server for ChatGPT", "submit to ChatGPT"
capabilities: Evaluates product fit for ChatGPT Apps using Know/Do/Show framework
Generates complete project templates with MCP server code
Creates React widget components with Apps SDK patterns
Implements authentication flows with OAuth integration
author: BayramAnnakov
securitySummary: This skill is a legitimate documentation and template builder for ChatGPT Apps. The static analyzer flagged 1322 potential issues, but all findings are false positives or benign patterns. The code uses secure crypto (crypto.randomUUID), properly blocks SSRF targets (AWS metadata), and contains only standard development patterns (env vars, fetch calls) documented in reference markdown files.
valueStatement: This skill guides developers through creating ChatGPT Apps from concept to App Store submission. It provides templates, implementation patterns, and testing workflows for Apps using the MCP protocol.
userTitle: Build ChatGPT Apps with Apps SDK
limitations: Does not execute code or deploy apps automatically
Requires users to configure their own API credentials
Does not provide hosting or infrastructure
Template code requires customization for production use

useCases.title: Build new ChatGPT Apps
useCases.description: Create production-ready ChatGPT Apps with widgets and MCP servers for the App Store

useCases.title: Add MCP Integration
useCases.description: Connect existing APIs to ChatGPT Apps using MCP protocol and OAuth authentication

useCases.title: Test App Implementations
useCases.description: Validate ChatGPT Apps locally using MCP Inspector and golden prompt testing

promptTemplates.title: Create new ChatGPT App
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new ChatGPT App called [AppName] that connects to [API] and displays [data type] with actions to [actions]

promptTemplates.title: Add OAuth Authentication
promptTemplates.prompt: Add OAuth authentication to my ChatGPT App with [AuthProvider] for user-specific data access

promptTemplates.title: Build Widget UI
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a widget that displays [content type] with filtering by [fields] and action buttons for [operations]

promptTemplates.title: Deploy to Production
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me prepare my ChatGPT App for App Store submission with proper health endpoints, CORS, and security headers

bestPractices: Follow the Know/Do/Show framework to ensure your app provides unique value beyond text responses
Use one job per tool principle and annotate tools with readOnlyHint, destructiveHint, and openWorldHint
Implement rate limiting and SSRF protection for production deployments
antiPatterns: Do not use separate paths for GET and POST MCP endpoints
Avoid hardcoding credentials in source files
Do not skip validation of image URLs in image proxy

faq.question: What is the Know/Do/Show framework?
faq.answer: A fit evaluation model where Know provides unique data, Do takes real actions, and Show displays rich UI beyond text.

faq.question: Does this skill deploy my app?
faq.answer: No, this skill generates templates and guides. You deploy using your chosen hosting provider like Fly.io or Vercel.

faq.question: What authentication does it support?
faq.answer: OAuth 2.0 with examples for Auth0, Stytch, and custom providers using the well-known endpoints pattern.

faq.question: Can I test locally before deploying?
faq.answer: Yes, use ngrok to create an HTTPS tunnel and MCP Inspector for local testing before connecting to ChatGPT.

faq.question: What UI patterns are supported?
faq.answer: List views, action buttons, loading states, dark mode, and responsive layouts using Apps SDK UI design tokens.

faq.question: How do I submit to the App Store?
faq.answer: Complete the pre-submission checklist including organization verification, tool annotations, and test credentials documentation.
